commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens quiet channel-owned progress callback forwarding beyond direct chats, preserves room-event suppression when source delivery is suppressed, and adds dispatch regression coverage.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +128. Total +128 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection. Current main still requires `chatType === "direct"` for quiet native progress forwarding, and the PR adds focused tests for the affected group/source-suppressed paths; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is needed; the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance or stronger Telegram-visible proof for the widened shared-room progress behavior.

target.title: fix(dispatch): forward channel-owned progress callbacks in all chat types when verbose is off
target.body:
## Summary

- Forward quiet channel-owned progress callbacks in group/channel sessions, not only direct chats.
- Keep default text tool summaries and `onToolResult` suppressed under `/verbose off`.
- Add regression coverage for the source-delivery-suppressed group path used by native preview lanes.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t 'channel-owned group progress callbacks|room-event progress callbacks|direct native progress callbacks'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/verbose off` no longer suppresses channel-owned progress callbacks before Telegram can update its native preview lane.

Real environment tested: Telegram bot-to-bot QA group, SUT gateway from this PR checkout, mock OpenAI-compatible provider for deterministic model text.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node /home/obviyus/.codex/skills/custom/telegram-e2e-bot-to-bot/scripts/run-mock-sut-e2e.mjs --gateway-port 19989 --mock-port 19992 --text '@{sut} Please answer with OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_{run} only.' --expect OPENCLAW_E2E_OK --timeout-ms 90000`

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the Telegram bot-to-bot run:

```text
driverMessageId: 22134
sutReplyMessageId: 22135
mockRequests: 1
matched: OPENCLAW_E2E_OK
```

Observed result after fix: SUT replied in-thread with `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK`; the request reached the model turn path through Telegram.

What was not tested: live Slack. Discord live proof is attached in an existing PR comment.

Closes #87612
